claim_policy: Every publication claim must resolve to repo_evidence, external_evidence, planned_work, or blocker status. Fixture-bounded evidence must not be described as full-corpus evidence.

claims.claim: The repository implements a modular NLP pipeline with ingestion, schema emission, downstream integration, storage/search, release, and quality tracks.
claims.publication_wording: Implemented repository-side pipeline components are documented by completed track artifacts and tests.

claims.claim: The repository records standards coverage for Akoma Ntoso, PROV-O, FOAF/SIOC, Wikidata, ontology mapping, Axiom-compatible source identity, and downstream publication outputs.
claims.publication_wording: Standards coverage is repository-evidenced where artifacts and tests exist, and is otherwise reported as planned or blocked.

claims.claim: The repository emits source-grounded bridge metadata for downstream rules-as-code consumers while keeping executable RuleSpec semantics out of the NLP package.
claims.publication_wording: Rules-as-code output is a non-executable, source-grounded bridge for downstream projects.

claims.claim: Ontology coverage, ontology mapping, mapping inference, and New Zealand-specific ontology candidates are recorded as checked-in data artifacts.
claims.publication_wording: Ontology claims are bounded to checked-in coverage, mapping, inference, and candidate artifacts.

claims.claim: Whole-corpus descriptive statistics are currently fixture-bounded unless supplied PipelineRecord Parquet exports are provided.
claims.publication_wording: The statistics report is deterministic and fixture-bounded; full-corpus claims require external Parquet inputs.

claims.claim: Graph, vector, and network analysis is currently fixture-bounded unless full graph exports and vector indexes are supplied.
claims.publication_wording: The graph/vector report is deterministic and fixture-bounded; full graph/vector claims require canonical exports.

claims.claim: A fresh checkout can reproduce repository-side checks with the documented pixi, pytest, ruff, docs, benchmark, and GitHub Actions workflows.
claims.publication_wording: Repository-side reproducibility is CI-backed; external corpora, credentials, and live services remain outside the default offline run.

claims.claim: The repository has publication-ready documentation scaffolding and analysis artifacts, while user-facing exploration and manuscript production remain later tracks.
claims.publication_wording: Publication protocol artifacts are present; figures, interactive exploration, and manuscript automation remain planned downstream work.

overclaim_guardrails: Use fixture-bounded for checked-in Track 32 and Track 33 reports.
Use repository-side for artifacts generated without full external corpora.
Use planned for Tracks 35-37 outputs until those tracks are complete.
Use blocker for full-corpus, live publication, authenticated API, or external-source claims without checked-in evidence.
